response:
We are given the matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-\frac{4}{3} & 4 & -\frac{11}{6} \\
-\frac{13}{3} & \frac{23}{6} & -\frac{25}{6} \\
\frac{5}{3} & -\frac{2}{3} & -\frac{5}{3}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this 3×3 matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use the cofactor expansion along the first row

The determinant of a 3×3 matrix:

$$
\det(A) = a_{11} \cdot \det\begin{pmatrix} b_{22} & b_{23} \\ b_{32} & b_{33} \end{pmatrix}
- a_{12} \cdot \det\begin{pmatrix} b_{21} & b_{23} \\ b_{31} & b_{33} \end{pmatrix}
+ a_{13} \cdot \det\begin{pmatrix} b_{21} & b_{22} \\ b_{31} & b_{32} \end{pmatrix}
$$

Where:
- $ a_{11} = -\frac{4}{3} $
- $ a_{12} = 4 $
- $ a_{13} = -\frac{11}{6} $
- The submatrices are:
  - $ \begin{pmatrix} \frac{23}{6} & -\frac{25}{6} \\ -\frac{2}{3} & -\frac{5}{3} \end{pmatrix} $
  - $ \begin{pmatrix} -\frac{13}{3} & -\frac{25}{6} \\ \frac{5}{3} & -\frac{5}{3} \end{pmatrix} $
  - $ \begin{pmatrix} -\frac{13}{3} & \frac{23}{6} \\ \frac{5}{3} & -\frac{2}{3} \end{pmatrix} $
